changelog:
Usage reconciliation + authoritative context window + 429 log clarity.

=== Why ===

Two client-visible defects: (1) the local prompt-cache estimator (promptCacheTracker.Compute) could emit cache_creation/cache_read token counts that EXCEED the real resolved input total (observed live: inputTokens=322495 but cache_creation=444208). billedClaudeInputTokens then floored input_tokens to 0 AND the emitted usage block (billed + cache_read + cache_creation) over-reported the true total, so a client's running context tally drifted upward every turn and could blow past 100%. (2) The context window was guessed from the model version string instead of the value Kiro actually reports, and context-usage percentages were forwarded unclamped.

=== Change - usage reconciliation (proxy/cache_tracker.go, proxy/handler.go) ===

- New reconcileCacheUsage(inputTokens, usage) caps cache_read then cache_creation so the invariant billed + cache_read + cache_creation == inputTokens always holds, preferring to keep cache_read (a concrete prefix match) over cache_creation (the per-turn estimate), and rescaling the 5m/1h creation breakdown proportionally to the capped creation value.
- Wired into BOTH emission paths: streaming via buildClaudeUsageMap (when includeCache), and the non-streaming Claude path in handler.go before resp.Usage.* is assigned.

=== Change - authoritative context window (proxy/handler.go, proxy/kiro.go) ===

- Context window is now sourced from Kiro's reported tokenLimits.maxInputTokens (contextWindowFromTokenLimits / Handler.contextWindowForModel) instead of being parsed from the model version. getContextWindowSize is now a pure 200000 fallback for when Kiro reports nothing.
- /v1/models emits the real Kiro window per model. OnContextUsage callbacks back-convert pct to absolute tokens using the per-model window.
- clampPercent bounds context-usage percentage to [0,100] at the parse source so a malformed/saturated upstream frame can never synthesize an input-token count outside [0, window].

=== Change - 429 log clarity (proxy/kiro.go) ===

- QuotaError.Error() now reads "rate limited (HTTP 429) on <endpoints>" instead of "quota exhausted ...", which conflated transient throttling with monthly OVERAGE quota exhaustion. The literal "429" is retained so isRetryableUpstreamError still classifies it as retryable-on-peer.
- The throttle failover loop logs "trying next endpoint" only when another endpoint remains.

=== Tests ===

- TestReconcileCacheUsage (invariant + the live overshoot case + edge cases), TestClampPercent, TestContextWindowFromTokenLimits, TestGetContextWindowSize, TestBuildAnthropicModelsResponseContextWindowFromKiro. Failover / account-disable classifier fixtures updated to the new 429 wording.
